title: Catalog updates dropping after search schema change
incident_summary: Catalog freshness degrades unevenly; the indexing pipeline looks healthy at first glance even though updates are being lost.

alerts.title: Index freshness backlog
alerts.content: Freshness lag exceeds 40 minutes for catalog updates.

alerts.title: Kafka lag advisory
alerts.content: Kafka lag slightly elevated but consumer throughput appears steady.

alerts.title: Query error low
alerts.content: Query error rates remain low despite freshness incidents.

logs.title: Schema mismatch drops
logs.content: index-writer dropping update batches: field sku_tags type mismatch.

logs.title: Pipeline health green
logs.content: pipeline health checker still green due to missing drop metric binding.

logs.title: Schema migration started
logs.content: migration job search_schema_v42 started before freshness lag rise.

runbook_snippets.title: Schema rollback and replay
runbook_snippets.content: Rollback index schema version and replay update topic from checkpoint.

runbook_snippets.title: Scale consumers
runbook_snippets.content: Scaling consumers is insufficient when records are dropped by schema mismatch.

runbook_snippets.title: Query cache flush
runbook_snippets.content: Cache flush can improve stale reads temporarily but will not restore dropped index updates.

timeline_notes.title: Migration approval
timeline_notes.content: Search schema migration approved and deployed at 07:15 UTC.

timeline_notes.title: Catalog sync event
timeline_notes.content: Catalog sync ran as usual and completed successfully.

red_herrings: Kafka lag warning and green health checks suggest capacity issue instead of schema-based drops.
Low query error hides data freshness regression.
true_root_cause: Schema migration caused index writer to drop update batches
true_root_cause_aliases: index pipeline schema mismatch dropping records
search schema v42 regression in writer
true_mitigation: Rollback schema migration and replay index update stream from checkpoint
true_mitigation_aliases: revert index schema and replay kafka updates
undo migration then reprocess dropped batches
